directive_questions_answered.C-Q1_dedicated_mode_field: ANSWERED — recommend commission_type='compression' field (not body-detection), aligning with PROP-043 architecture
directive_questions_answered.C-Q2_minimum_HNOTE_schema: ANSWERED — required: commission, commission_type, target_agents, target_win_id/target_section_id, target_field, target_word_count, hold_for_operator_review, subject, body. Strongly recommended: preservation_list (verdict_must_remain, claim_must_remain_verbatim, load_bearing_numbers, load_bearing_citations, cross_win_refs_must_remain, structural_section_refs_must_remain), allowed_cuts, allowed_link_out_destinations, hard_cap_word_count, follow_up_iss_allowed. Includes worked example matching EXP-401.
directive_questions_answered.C-Q3_one_surface_per_HNOTE: ANSWERED — recommend one surface per HNOTE (invariant simplicity; per-surface operator review; FIFO throughput equivalence). Future amendment path defined for parent-batch HNOTEs if friction emerges.
directive_questions_answered.C-Q4_cross_agent_commissions: ANSWERED — analyst-exclusive. Curmudgeon and decider channels not used for compression commissions; channel-of-record discipline.
directive_questions_answered.D-Q1_HOLD_queue_vs_parallel_preview: ANSWERED — HOLD queue at launch (non-negotiable per operator preference). Parallel curmudgeon preview deferred as future relaxation.
directive_questions_answered.D-Q2_integration_additive_fields: ANSWERED — additive lifecycle fields on EXP (status one-way pending-operator-review → integrated|rejected|revising, plus integrated_at, integrated_by, integration_commit_sha, operator_approval_hnote_id, follow_up_iss_filed). Tracker additive: integrated=true, integrated_at, integration_mode='compression_operator_approved'. Post-integration curmudgeon queue push with class='verification'.
directive_questions_answered.D-Q3_followup_iss_auto_file_vs_operator_: ANSWERED — surface to operator. Decider does NOT auto-file; operator signals via approval HNOTE's follow_up_iss_decision field.
directive_questions_answered.D-Q4_anchor_normalization: ANSWERED — both: decider normalizes #partN.html#X → #X at integration time AND test.js stays as backstop. Compression-scoped at launch; expansion to all integrations deferred.
directive_questions_answered.Q1_dispatcher_placement_option: ANSWERED — Option C (delegate to PROP-043 Mode 0.5 with commission_type='compression'). New Mode 6 (Option A) and generalize-Mode-5 (Option B) rejected.
directive_questions_answered.Q2_priority_relative_to_mode_5: N/A under Option C — Mode 0.5 FIFO handles all commissions equally
directive_questions_answered.Q4_option_C_implications: Confirmed — PROP-042 stays tightly scoped to HNOTE-schema + EXP-shape + decider-intake; no dispatcher prompt changes
directive_questions_answered.Q5_sequencing_with_dispatcher_fix: ANSWERED — PROP-043 first, PROP-042 second; ship together in coordinated review session per Q-OP-A042-1

details.interpretation: Pipeline is in excellent shape post-PROP-031/PROP-027/PROP-029 burndown work. Closure velocity (132/7d) overwhelmingly exceeds creation velocity (7/7d), net +125 issues closed in last week. Single open-status item, only one item older than 30 days, 33 items in assigned-analyst (well-handled by Mode 1 / baby drain). The baby_drain_count_7d=1 and verify_drain_count_7d=0 reflect that the heavy backlog clearance is happening via decider BAU/burndown auto-closure (closure-ledger.jsonl entries) rather than via baby or verify-mode work — consistent with the project being in 'burndown-just-completed, BAU steady state' phase. No findings to escalate.
